title: calytrix drummondii
intro: Calytrix drummondii, also known as the Drummond's bustard pea, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Calytrix drummondii has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and providing relief from respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion for its medicinal uses, and in some cases, it is also used as a poultice or applied topically to the affected area.
